title: How to install podman as a service in MAC OS Server
body:
Whenever the MACOS server restarted, the podman need to manually start again via command

```bash
podman machine start

```

Can I install as a brew service which able to start/stop? how can i do that?

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out, @Taymindis.

I'll ping the Mac OS experts who may know how to do that. @ashley-cui @benoitf @baude @rhatdan @gbraad

Thank you for the heads up. There are several ways this can be approached: let podman desktop handle this by means of the tray visibility, or set up a way for podman itself to handle this. Perhaps some other way? Suggestions welcome. Curious how Ashley and Florent see this.

@gbraad, thanks!  It sounds nice to have Podman handle it and let Podman-Desktop use it?

On Linux, I'd just use systemd for it :^)

On macos we can use launchd, however the model for windows would be slightly more involved. Centralizing this responsibility in podman sounds better to me to, as that allows Desktop to only handle a flag for containers, instead of the initialization of the engine.

On Podman Desktop, on macOS, we'll allow to launch Podman machine each time we launch Podman Desktop (same lifecycle. So quite easy to start as you just click on an icon and see status/etc).
And next, to allow to Start Podman Desktop on every start. (so by mixing all options you can achieve what you want).
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@n1hility @benoitf @gbraad what is the state of this now?

Podman Desktop has a PR about that https://github.com/containers/podman-desktop/pull/510

ie: start Podman Desktop (that starts Podman Machine) on boot for macOS
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
